revision.text:
[[ja:網浜茶臼山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Aminohama Chausuyama Kofun |image = [[File:Aminohama Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the Kofun (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |location = {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|en|Naka-ku, Okayama|qid=Q852116|lt=Naka Ward, Okayama|1=Naka-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|qid=Q200078|lt=Okayama City|1=Okayama}}, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34.6455|N|133.942172|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |size = Mound length: 92m
Rear height: 8m |built = Early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} |burial = Assumed {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} |burial goods = {{ill|Special ceremonial clay stands and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special ceremonial clay stands and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} |status = Not Historic Site1 as a historic site |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Aminohama Chausuyama Kofun''' (網浜茶臼山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|en|Naka-ku, Okayama|qid=Q852116|lt=Naka Ward, Okayama|1=Naka-ku, Okayama}}, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The site is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated on the southwestern tip of the [[Misaoyama]] Hills, an independent massif to the east of Okayama City, this tumulus commands views of the city center, Kojima Bay, and the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388656}}.神宮寺山古墳 網浜茶臼山古墳 (2007), pp. 24–37. Its location overlaps with the historic Higashiyama Cemetery, resulting in significant alterations to the mound. The Kofun features a keyhole type with the front part facing east. Traces of pebble-like covering stones and {{ill|Special ceremonial clay stands and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special ceremonial clay stands and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} have been discovered on the surface. The burial chamber is assumed to be a pit-style stone chamber, with andesite slabs repurposed as cemetery retaining walls. Based on the unearthed burial goods, the kofun is estimated to date back to the early Kofun period. It is considered one of the oldest leadership tumuli in the Asahi River basin, alongside {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun 109|ja|操山109号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun 109|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Chronology == * '''1930:''' First recorded in the publication ''History of Okayama Prefecture (Part 1)'' as "Aminohama Hilltop Kofun." * '''1965:''' Identified as a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a mound length of 83 meters in ''Misaoyama Histories 14.'' * '''1990:''' Survey conducted by Masamasa Ugaki. * '''1993:''' Investigation during cemetery reorganization by Okayama City Board of Education. == area == Surveyed in 1990, the mound is measured as follows: * '''Overall length:''' 92 meters * '''Rear mound:''' Diameter 56.5 meters, height 8 meters * '''Front mound:''' Possible fanned type, height 4.5 meters Among the tumuli in the Misaoyama Hills, it is the third largest, following {{ill|Kanakurayama Kofun|ja|金蔵山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanakurayama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Minato Chausuyama Kofun|ja|湊茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Harvid|網浜茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/5301 |title=神宮寺山古墳 網浜茶臼山古墳|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education|pages=24–37|ref={{Harvid|神宮寺山古墳 網浜茶臼山古墳|2007|qq=}}|qq=}} - Available at the Nara Historic Site Research Institute. * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=網浜茶臼山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[Heibonsha|ja|平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|網浜茶臼山古墳（平凡社）|1988|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masamasa Ugaki|year=1990|chapter=網浜茶臼山古墳・操山109号墳の測量調査 -吉備の前期古墳III-|title=古代吉備 第12集|publisher=Ancient Kibi Research Association|ref=|qq=}} == See Also == * [[Kanakurayama Kofun|Kinzanzan Kofun]] * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|version=99995724|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:網浜茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55522478}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]]
